_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Infants with critical congenital heart defects that are dependent on the patency of the ductus arteriosus (PDA) for survival, categorized into three groups:

Duct dependent Pulmonary Circulation: Conditions where pulmonary circulation is dependent on patency of ductus arteriosus (e.g., pulmonary atresia, critical RVOT/pulmonary stenosis, severe Ebstein's anomaly).

Duct dependent systemic circulation: Conditions where systemic circulation is dependent on patency of ductus arteriosus (e.g., hypoplastic left heart syndrome, interrupted aortic arch, critical LVOT obstruction, critical coarctation).
Admixture Lesions: TGA with ductus as only site for mixing of circulation.
Progressive acidosis, weak/impalpable leg pulses, or oliguria due to renal impairment if ductus constricts in these conditions.

claim.label: Per-procedure or Post procedure stills of angio or ECHO showing stent in place, with report

ppd_preauth.q: Are clinical notes detailed with history, signs, symptoms, and indication for procedure?
Was the Echo report or Angiography report suggestive of duct-dependent congenital heart disease?

cpd_claim.q: Does the Per procedure/ Post procedure still of angio/ ECHO show stent placement in PDA?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Echo report or Angiography report not indicative of duct-dependent congenital heart disease.
Clinical notes lack detailed history, signs, symptoms, or indication for the procedure.
Procedure/Operative notes missing or not detailed.
Per-procedure or Post-procedure stills (angio/ECHO) not submitted or not clearly showing stent placement in PDA.
Detailed Discharge Summary missing or does not mention date of follow-up.
Invoice or barcode of the stent used is missing.

clinical_key_pointers: These conditions require ductal patency to maintain perfusion either for pulmonary circulation or for complete or partial (lower part of body) systemic circulation.

If ductus constricts, progressive acidosis develops, leg pulses become weak, impalpable and oliguria develop due to renal impairment and become progressively compromised. Therefore, stenting of ductus is done till definitive management.
Clinical Features: Duct-dependent Systemic circulation presents with hypoxia and respiratory distress, severe heart failure, absent femoral pulses, severe metabolic acidosis.
Clinical Features: Duct-dependent Pulmonary circulation presents with cyanosis, tachypnoea, normal pulses, improvement in systemic saturation on 100% oxygen inhalation.
Success of the procedure depends on careful case selection based on the angiographic morphology of the ductus.
